title: koeleria villosa subsp. longiglumis
intro: Koeleria villosa subsp. longiglumis is a subspecies of the Koeleria villosa grass, commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America, known for its tall, slender stems and feathery inflorescences. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Koeleria villosa subsp. longiglumis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
